museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: August 2015: Gamescom and late-summer previews point toward Metal Gear, Fallout and the Christmas shelf.
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for August 2015. Regional timing and UK retail context should be checked before verification.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: Evolve
items.body: A co-op monster hunt arrives carrying the early live-service vocabulary of the generation.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for February 10, 2015.

items.title: Cities: Skylines
items.body: A city-builder becomes the PC comfort object SimCity had failed to be.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 10, 2015.

items.title: Bloodborne
items.body: FromSoftware makes PS4 feel dangerous, elegant and essential.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies between March 24 and March 27; UK/PAL exact date should be checked.

items.title: Mortal Kombat X
items.body: NetherRealm brings fighting-game spectacle into the share-button era.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for April 14, 2015.

items.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
items.body: CD Projekt Red turns a huge RPG into a cultural reference point.
items.sourceNote: CD Projekt and release references list May 19, 2015.

items.title: Splatoon
items.body: Nintendo invents a colourful online shooter with its own grammar.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 2015; regional exact date should be checked.

items.title: Batman: Arkham Knight
items.body: Rocksteady closes its Batman trilogy while the PC version becomes a cautionary tale.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for June 23, 2015.

items.title: Rocket League
items.body: Car football becomes a PS Plus and Twitch word-of-mouth miracle.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for July 7, 2015.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: Nintendo announces DeNA partnership and NX
items.body: Nintendo signals both mobile plans and a future dedicated-games platform during a difficult Wii U period.
items.sourceNote: DeNA/Nintendo announcement is dated March 17, 2015.

items.title: PS4 gains generation momentum
items.body: Bloodborne, The Witcher 3, Batman, Metal Gear and Fallout make PS4 feel increasingly central on UK shelves.
items.sourceNote: Year-level release context; verify market-share claims before final copy.

items.title: Xbox One leans on bundles and services
items.body: Microsoft's console fights through exclusives, backwards compatibility talk and pricing rather than clear cultural dominance.
items.sourceNote: 2015 platform context; exact UK retail bundle claims need further period sources.

items.title: Steam living-room ambitions remain uncertain
items.body: Steam Machines and controller talk make PC-in-the-living-room feel possible but not settled.
items.sourceNote: Late-2015 Steam Machine context should be verified against Valve/OEM sources.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Streams and servers
items.body: Online life is Twitch clips, YouTube criticism, Steam reviews, Reddit theories and PSN/Xbox Live parties becoming routine.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Services and stores
items.body: Rocket League shows how a PS Plus launch and streaming word of mouth can make a game feel instantly communal.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Forums and feeds
items.body: Undertale proves that Tumblr, YouTube, fan art and music sharing can turn a small PC RPG into a cultural weather system.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: Patches, DLC plans and server issues are now part of how players judge a game, not afterthoughts.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like the modern backlog hardened into place. Every month seemed to bring another game too large to finish cleanly.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: The Witcher 3, Fallout 4 and Metal Gear made scale feel seductive and exhausting at the same time.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: Nintendo's year had grief in it. Splatoon and Mario Maker showed invention, while Iwata's passing changed the emotional tone around the company.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: In the UK, the ritual was split between boxed preorders, supermarket deals, PS Plus downloads, Steam sales and YouTube tabs left open after midnight.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
